floor_history.headline: fable-blitz deep recon: csmd4 IS marionation region-1's cheaper twin (no region-3) - the whole gap is the in-place-basin v1/a0 allocation-equation flip (pri(arg5) > pri(t0)); the wrap-weighting x in-p

Structural axis (decl order, s32/u32 narrow, statement re-assoc under arg5-after-t0) closed directly on csmd4: 4/4 masked-INERT at 2, head-order flip regresses to 7 - twin's structural closure confirm

Structural axis on csmd4 h5 base fully closed: 13 variants swept (var-splits, expression compression, decl-position, pointer-deref form) — 8 INERT at masked 2, 5 measured regressions (t0s-split=3, arg

s13 permuter+structural: h5-multexpander fresh-seed 2999 iters 0-novel (base=60); 3 KILLED — D_800F19C0 fn-body-hoist +13, g3+multi-set VALUE carrier for arg5 INERT (both *idx_1495 and t0 seeds).

frontier.hypothesis: PERM_* directed mutation on the pointer-arith node `(u8*)tbl_125c + t0` may reach mutation forms permuter random misses due to the perm_add_sub AssertionError. Candidate hints: staged-value-reused-variable, register-alloc-pure-c.
frontier.mechanism: tools/permuter_annotate.py --func cpu_side_move_dir_4 --hint <slug> writes PERM_*-annotated candidates that force the permuter to explore specific transformations on flagged nodes, bypassing the random-mutation blindspot. The h5-multexpander base has base_score=60 vs g3's 40; a directed campaign on the h5 pointer-arith node has not been run.
frontier.next_probe: python3 tools/permuter_annotate.py --list-hints; select a hint aligned to the frontier (staged-value-reused-variable is directly applicable to the h5 t0-chain); write annotated candidate to tmp/permuter_candidates/cpu_side_move_dir_4.c; tools/permuter_campaign.py launch --func cpu_side_move_dir_4 --dir tmp/perm_csmd4_directed --label s14_h5_directed -j 8 --stop-on-zero. Fresh-seed 20-30 min window.

frontier.hypothesis: The h5-multexpander chassis has 5 structural axes fully closed (block-local structure s3-s5, do-while(0) 4 scopes s4-s5, m2c rederive s8, marionation transplants s9, arg5_addr two-SETs s11-s12) plus fn-body-scope D_800F19C0-hoist KILLED (s13). The remaining untested fn-body-scope moves are: hoisting tbl_125c/idx_1494/idx_1495/D_800F19BC inits into do_timeout block, or moving the `cnt = D_800F19BC; D_800F19BC = cnt - -1;` counter increment PAST the do_timeout label.
frontier.mechanism: Same fn-body-scope-move mechanism as frontier #1 (LUID at expand + qty birth-order); each remaining prologue-init is a candidate for the same probe. WIP explicitly warned the file-level s-reg web is coupled to these initializers.
frontier.next_probe: Sweep the remaining prologue-init hoists into do_timeout individually: (a) tbl_125c into do_timeout, (b) idx_1494 into do_timeout, (c) idx_1495 into do_timeout, (d) D_800F19BC=0 into do_timeout. Each is a single sandbox measurement. All four are structural pure-C moves within pure-C. If any is INERT/improved, seed a permuter chassis on it.

frontier.hypothesis: The decomp.me corpus scrape via tools/decomp_me_scrape.py against asm/funcs/cpu_side_move_dir_4.s remains gated on operator installing curl_cffi in .venv (unchanged from s9/s11/s12). A structurally-novel-in-corpus C form from a similar-asm scratch could bypass the search space closed by s1-s13.
frontier.mechanism: Coarse asm-overlap pre-filter over the downloaded decomp.me corpus (BB2 toolchain class: gcc2.7.2-psx / gcc2.7.2-cdk / psyq3.5); manual inspection of top hits could reveal an original-source shape our block-local + fn-body-scope search space missed.
frontier.next_probe: Operator installs curl_cffi in .venv (`source .venv/bin/activate && pip install curl_cffi`); next rederive session runs `python3 tools/decomp_me_scrape.py search --asm-file asm/funcs/cpu_side_move_dir_4.s` and manually inspects top asm-overlap hits.
